items.title: Leaked documents reveal doubts over BHP's ability to hit net zero

children.content: The mining giant's delay in the rollout of renewable energy and a fleet of electric trucks and trains in the Pilbara has raised doubts inside the company about it meeting its ambitious climate goal.

articles.title: BHP talked up its green plans for WA. Leaked documents show it then shelved them

children.content: Hundreds of pages of internal BHP documents reveal how the company sold itself as a climate leader while internally finding reasons to put off action in the Pilbara.

image.altText: A collage graphic showing a mining truck, wind turbines, solar panels a train and excerpts from documents.

gallery.altText: A large yellow mining truck drives along a raise road. It has a load in its tray.

thumbnail.altText: A large yellow mining truck drives along a raise road. It has a load in its tray.

items.title: Iran says 'no tolls' on Strait of Hormuz, but warns everything has a cost

children.content: While on assignment inside Iran, our reporter asked a foreign ministry official about the regime's plan for the Strait of Hormuz. The spokesperson responded that "there is no toll" but also signalled crossing the strait will not be entirely free.

image.altText: ABC reporter Karishma Vyas sits among other reporters and asks a question

items.title: Trump demands Arab nations recognise Israel as part of Iran war deal

children.content: Donald Trump has boldly tied negotiations to end his war with Iran to a swathe of Arab nations formally establishing diplomatic ties with Israel.

image.altText: Israeli PM Netanyahu shakes hands with US President Donald Trump, who points at him.

items.title: Labor to ignore call for pause on CGT changes

children.content: The government is pushing ahead with capital gains tax changes while consulting on limited carve-outs.

image.altText: Prime Minister Anthony Albanese in a grey suit, staring to his right, with the Australian flag behind him.

items.title: Xi served Putin and Trump Peking duck. Albanese missed out

children.content: Xi Jinping has learned that every ingredient placed on a state table is a word in a diplomatic sentence.

image.altText: A man raises a glass of champagne from behind a lectern.

items.title: Missing man's ex-girlfriend feared being 'jumped'

children.content: The former girlfriend of missing Queensland man Jack McLennan says she held fears for her safety because of rumours surrounding his disappearance.

gallery.altText: Jack McLennan and Carlee Beetham pictured happy together in front of a waterfall

thumbnail.altText: Jack McLennan and Carlee Beetham pictured happy together in front of a waterfall

items.title: Rate hikes and tax changes have broken the back of the latest property boom

children.content: Early signs of an Australian housing market correction are now clearly visible, and at the heart of the recent property market weakness is less vendor power.

image.altText: An overhead shot of a housing estate.

items.title: 'Intolerable': When $768m bridge was being designed, engineers raised a red flag

children.content: There are fresh concerns over the structural integrity of Hobart's new Bridgewater Bridge, which one expert who worked on the project suggested could eventually lead to a need to reduce traffic flow or speed limits.

image.altText: A silver metal device attached to a concrete pylon of a bridge.

items.title: After losing her 3yo daughter, one mum says MDMA therapy saved her life

children.content: Jess Yugovich is among the Australians trying MDMA-assisted psychotherapy after being diagnosed with PTSD, as highly regulated facilities process the drug for therapeutic use.

gallery.altText: Woman with short brown hair looks at camera in a green silk shirt and in a well lit therapy room.

thumbnail.altText: Woman with short brown hair looks at camera in a green silk shirt and in a well lit therapy room.

items.title: From 'the Reverend' to the 'Big Freeze', Neale Daniher never stopped leading

children.content: Neale Daniher showed leadership on and off the field throughout his life, from club captaincy to coaching and as Australian of the Year — but no more so when facing the challenge of an MND diagnosis.

image.altText: Former AFL player and coach Neale Daniher stands smiling on a stage in front of hundreds of Melbourne fans.

items.title: NACC chief Brereton to face Senate grilling after shock resignation

children.content: National Anti-Corruption Commissioner Paul Brereton will face a grilling by senators at estimates hearings today following his shock resignation on Monday, amid calls for a wider reset of the troubled agency.

image.altText: Paul Brereton speaks during a committee hearing.

items.title: One world record and a whole lot of regret for Enhanced Games athletes

children.content: Despite the drugs and the super suits, the Enhanced Games delivered little more than a smattering of PBs and a single world fastest time for the hefty price of an athlete's hard-earned credibility.

gallery.altText: Confetti rains down on a pool
Fred Kerley runs with his head tilted

thumbnail.altText: Confetti rains down on a pool
Fred Kerley runs with his head tilted

items.title: Flexible work broader than just working from home

children.content: A bill amending the Fair Work Act 2009 to include the right to work from home is too narrow in its focus and could lead to discrimination, according to submissions in a federal inquiry.

image.altText: A woman sitting at her desk working from home.

items.title: Ebola outbreak outpacing health response, WHO chief says

children.content: It comes as health facilities in some of the worst-hit regions are attacked amid ongoing violence.

image.altText: Five health care workers wearing PPE, facemasks and eyemasks standing lined up out the front of a home.

items.title: In 2024, Richard Gadd gave us Baby Reindeer. He follows it up with Half Man

children.content: After Baby Reindeer — about a comedian and his stalker — became a surprise hit, Richard Gadd ignored the pressure to replicate his success, making the show he couldn't stop thinking about.

gallery.altText: A TV still of Richard Gadd, 37, with a beard and short back and sides, in a leather jacket, eyeballing the camera.

thumbnail.altText: A TV still of Richard Gadd, 37, with a beard and short back and sides, in a leather jacket, eyeballing the camera.

avatar.alt: Hannah Story, a blonde woman sitting outside smiles. A dog is at her feet, and she wears a T-shirt that reads "MIRANDA JULY".

items.title: How and how not to use AI for help with your finances

children.content: "Hallucinations", privacy and other factors to consider if you’re turning to artificial intelligence financial advice.

image.altText: The hands hands of a woman typing, while she's reclined on the sofa.

items.title: Flag quiz: Which country has the most purple on its flag?

children.content: What's the name of the solo star on the Australian flag? And can you tell the difference between the Russian and Dutch flags?

image.altText: A photo of the Australian flag waving in a blue sky.

items.title: 'Fool, ignoramus': Parliamentary committee suspended as senators clash

children.content: A heated exchange prompted a Liberal senator to step in and call point of order on the Labor chair after several insults directed at Nationals senator Bridget McKenzie, before a brief suspension.

items.title: Labor calls for spare change as One Nation surges

children.content: Federal Labor is asking for donations to fight One Nation as the latest polling shows the minor party continuing to surge in key electorates, including in Western Sydney.

image.altText: A woman stands in a crowd, waving.

items.title: What we know about the remaining families returning from Syria

children.content: The last cohort of ISIS-linked Australians living in a notorious Syrian camp are expected to return back into Australia in the coming hours. Here's what we know about them.

image.altText: A bus driving down a road in the middle of a flat plain.

items.title: Push to find resting places for remains found in desecrated graves

children.content: Nine piles of dirt containing up to 5,000 human remains from dug-up graves have been dumped at the rear of a regional Victorian cemetery. A team of archaeologists is helping to find a home for the disturbed remains.

gallery.altText: Deb knelt next to a stone headstone.

thumbnail.altText: Deb knelt next to a stone headstone.

avatar.alt: A headshot of Nate standing in front of a dimly lit building wearing a jacket.

items.title: Community 'devastated' by plans for gas-powered data centres

children.content: More than 200 people voice their opposition to plans to build one of the country’s largest gas-fired power plants in the NSW Southern Highlands to provide energy for data centres.

image.altText: a line of people stand in rain gear on a field a child in the foreground hangs over a 'stop gas' sign

items.title: Thai cave rescuers work to free seven people trapped in flooded Laos cave

children.content: Seven villagers entered the cave in central Laos five days ago looking for gold when heavy rain caused flash flooding, trapping them inside.

image.altText: Four men stand at a narrow cave entrance wearing helmets and carrying ropes.

items.title: 'We pay our rates': Call for island to introduce public bus

children.content: Macleay Island residents say the community has a "very high percentage" of people who are elderly and have a disability, who would benefit from a bus service.

gallery.altText: Three women standing on the side of a street. They are on at the bottom of the hill.

thumbnail.altText: Three women standing on the side of a street. They are on at the bottom of the hill.

avatar.alt: A woman wearing a jacket sits in a park.

items.title: More than 20 hospitalised after substance sprayed in Tokyo mall

children.content: The shopping centre, located in the touristy and upmarket shopping district of Ginza, has been blocked off following the incident.

image.altText: People in hazmat suits inspect the outside of a building.

items.title: Renewables company destroys Indigenous shelter despite knowing about it

children.content: ACREZ apologises to traditional owners "without reservation" as the NSW Environment Minister Penny Sharpe expresses her "fury" at the incident.

image.altText: Landscape with wind turbines in distance

items.title: Crews to work overnight after burst pipe in Melbourne leaves thousands without power

children.content: A power company was forced to switch off power to about 10,000 customers in the heart of Melbourne after a burst water main threatened to flood a substation on Monday afternoon.

image.altText: Trucks pumping out thousands of litres of water from a flooded substation in Melbourne's CBD.

items.title: Dezi Freeman goaded officers to 'come and get me' before fatal shooting

children.content: The Coroners Court has heard about Dezi Freeman's final moments before a fatal confrontation at his hideout in Victoria's north-east.

image.altText: Screengrab from footage of Dezi Freeman wearing sunglasses and gesturing while speaking with police.

items.title: ASIO reviewed past terror cases in 2024 but didn't re-examine Bondi attackers

children.content: The head of Australia's domestic intelligence agency reveals it reviewed past terrorism investigations in 2024 which did not extend to a re-examination of the soon to be Bondi gunmen because of a resourcing decision.
